_comment: EXAMPLE MCP client config for the Kali MCP Server (Phase 3, Task 3.1).
Copy the relevant entry into your real client config (see CONNECTING.md for
exact paths), then restart the client. There are NO secrets in this file —
it stays an .example only so you copy it to the client's own location.
Both entries launch the SAME image (kali-mcp:phase1) and speak MCP over the
container's stdio (CLAUDE.md §4: stdio transport, no network port opened).
The ONLY difference is the container's network stack:
kali-mcp      — bridge/default (isolated). Loopback + self-tests. On bridge,
host-discovery to LAN hosts is filtered, so your own router
reads 'down' (NETWORKING.md). Use this unless you are
kali-mcp-lan  — real-LAN (host networking, --network host). The container
shares the host's network stack, so nmap/masscan/arp-scan
see your actual devices. EXPLICIT opt-in: it removes the
bridge isolation layer. Use ONLY for real network scans.
Both get --cap-add NET_RAW/NET_ADMIN (CLAUDE.md §4) so non-root raw-socket
scans/captures work; scope.py still gates every target to private ranges in
BOTH modes. Add only the entry you need — you do not need both.
